subject: Chemistry
question_text: A hydrogenation reaction is carried out at 500 K. If the same reaction is carried out in the presence of a catalyst at the same rate, the temperature required is 400 K. Calculate the activation energy of the reaction if the catalyst lowers the activation barrier by 20 kJ mol-1.

subject: Chemistry
micro_topic: Electrochemical Series Cell And Their Emf
question_text:
The following electrochemical cell has been set up.
Pt(1) | Fe3+, Fe2+ (a = 1) | Ce4+, Ce3+ (a=1) | Pt(2)
Eo (Fe3+, Fe2+) = 0.77 V; Eo (Ce4+, Ce3+) = 1.61 V
If an ammeter is connected between the two platinum electrodes, predict the direction of flow of current. Will the current increase or decrease with time?

subject: Chemistry
question_text: Copper sulphate solution (250 mL) was electrolysed using platinum anode and a copper cathode. A constant current of 2mA was passed for 16 minutes. It was found that after electrolysis the absorbance of the solution was reduced to 50% of its original value. Calculate the concentration of copper sulphate in the solution to begin with.

subject: Chemistry
micro_topic: Abnormal Colligative Property And Vant Hoff Factor
question_text: To 500 cm3 of water, 3.0 $$\times$$ 10-3 kg of acetic acid is added. If 23% of acetic acid is dissociated, what will be the depression in freezing point? Kf and density of water are 1.86 K kg-1 mol-1 and 0.997 g cm-3, respectively

subject: Chemistry
question_text: Write down the M.O. electron distribution of O2. Specify its bond order and magnetic property.

subject: Chemistry
question_text: Calculate the energy required to excite one litre of hydrogen gas at 1 atm and 298 K to the first excited state of atomic hydrogen. The energy for the dissociation of H-H bond 436 kJ mol-1.

subject: Mathematics
question_text: If $$\alpha ,\,\beta $$ are the roots of $$a{x^2} + bx + c = 0$$,  $$\,\left( {a \ne 0} \right)$$ and $$\alpha  + \delta ,\,\,\beta  + \delta $$ are the roots of $$A{x^2} + Bx + c = 0,$$   $$\left( {A \ne 0\,} \right)\,$$ for some contant $$\delta $$, then prove that $${{{b^2} - 4ac} \over {{a^2}}} = {{{B^2} - 4Ac} \over {{A^2}}}$$.

subject: Mathematics
micro_topic: Probability Distribution Of A Random Variable
question_text: A coin has probability $$p$$ of showing head when tossed. It is tossed $$n$$ times. Let $${p_n}$$ denote the probability that no two (or more) consecutive heads occur. Prove that $${p_1} = 1,{p_2} = 1 - {p^2}$$ and $${p_n} = \left( {1 - p} \right).\,\,{p_{n - 1}} + p\left( {1 - p} \right){p_{n - 2}}$$ for all $$n \ge 3.$$

subject: Mathematics
question_text:
For $$x>0,$$ let $$f\left( x \right) = \int\limits_e^x {{{\ln t} \over {1 + t}}dt.} $$ Find the function 
$$f\left( x \right) + f\left( {{1 \over x}} \right)$$ and show that $$f\left( e \right) + f\left( {{1 \over e}} \right) = {1 \over 2}.$$ 
Here, $$\ln t = {\log _e}t$$.

subject: Mathematics
question_text:
Suppose $$p\left( x \right) = {a_0} + {a_1}x + {a_2}{x^2} + .......... + {a_n}{x^n}.$$ If 
$$\left| {p\left( x \right)} \right| \le \left| {{e^{x - 1}} - 1} \right|$$ for all $$x \ge 0$$, prove that 
 $$\left| {{a_1} + 2{a_2} + ........ + n{a_n}} \right| \le 1$$.

subject: Mathematics
question_text:
Let $$ABC$$ be a triangle with incentre $$I$$ and inradius $$r$$. Let $$D,E,F$$ be the feet of the perpendiculars from $$I$$ to the sides $$BC$$, $$CA$$ and $$AB$$ respectively. If $${r_1},{r_2}$$ and $${r_3}$$ are the radii of circles inscribed in the quadrilaterals $$AFIE$$, $$BDIF$$ and $$CEID$$ respectively, prove that 
$$${{{r_1}} \over {r - {r_1}}} + {{{r_2}} \over {r - {r_2}}} + {{{r_3}} \over {r - {r_3}}} = {{{r_1}{r_2}{r_3}} \over {\left( {e - {r_1}} \right)\left( {r - {r_2}} \right)\left( {r - {r_3}} \right)}}$$$

subject: Mathematics
micro_topic: Question Based On Basic Definition And Parametric Representation
question_text: Let $${C_1}$$ and $${C_2}$$ be respectively, the parabolas $${x^2} = y - 1$$ and $${y^2} = x - 1$$. Let $$P$$ be any point on $${C_1}$$ and $$Q$$ be any point on $${C_2}$$. Let $${P_1}$$ and $${Q_1}$$ be the reflections of $$P$$ and $$Q$$, respectively, with respect to the line $$y=x$$. Prove that $${P_1}$$ lies on $${C_2}$$, $${Q_1}$$ lies on $${C_1}$$ and $$PQ \ge $$ min $$\left\{ {P{P_1},Q{Q_1}} \right\}$$. Hence or otherwise determine points $${P_0}$$ and $${Q_0}$$ on the parabolas $${C_1}$$ and $${C_2}$$ respectively such that $${P_0}{Q_0} \le PQ$$ for all pairs of points $$(P,Q)$$ with $$P$$ on  $${C_1}$$ and $$Q$$  on $${C_2}$$.

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Let $$ABC$$ be an equilateral triangle inscribed in the circle $${x^2} + {y^2} = {a^2}$$. Suppose perpendiculars from $$A, B, C$$ to the major axis of the ellipse $$x.{{{x^2}} \over {{a^2}}} + {{{y^2}} \over {{b^2}}} = 1$$, $$(a>b)$$ meets the ellipse respectively, at $$P, Q, R$$. so that $$P, Q, R$$ lie on the same side of the major axis as $$A, B, C$$ respectively. Prove that the normals to the ellipse drawn at the points $$P, Q$$ and $$R$$  are concurrent.

subject: Mathematics
topic: Straight Lines And Pair Of Straight Lines
question_text: Let $$ABC$$ and $$PQR$$ be any two triangles in the same plane. Assume that the prependiculars from the points $$A, B, C$$ to the sides $$QR, RP, PQ$$ respectively are concurrent. Using vector methods or otherwise, prove that the prependiculars from $$P, Q, R $$ to $$BC,$$ $$CA$$, $$AB$$ respectively are also concurrent.

subject: Mathematics
topic: Straight Lines And Pair Of Straight Lines
question_text: For points $$P\,\,\, = \left( {{x_1},\,{y_1}} \right)$$ and $$Q\,\,\, = \left( {{x_2},\,{y_2}} \right)$$ of the co-ordinate plane, a new distance $$d\left( {P,\,Q} \right)$$ is defined by $$d\left( {P,\,Q} \right)$$$$ = \left( {{x_2},\,{y_2}} \right)\left| {{x_1} - {x_2}} \right| + \left| {{y_1} - {y_2}} \right|.$$  Let $$O = (0, 0)$$  and $$A = (3, 2)$$. Prove that the set of points in the first quadrant which are equidistant (with respect to the new distance) from $$O$$ and $$A$$ consists of the union of a line segment of finite length and an infinite ray. Sketch this set in a labelled diagram.

subject: Mathematics
question_text: The fourth power of the common difference of an arithmatic progression with integer entries is added to the product of any four consecutive terms of it. Prove that the resulting sum is the square of an integer.

subject: Mathematics
question_text:
A coin probability $$p$$ of showing head when tossed. It is tossed $$n$$ times. Let $${p_n}$$ denote the probability that no two (or more) consecutive heads occur. Prove that $${p_1} = 1,\,\,{p_2} = 1 - {p^2}$$ and $${p_n} = \left( {1 - p} \right).\,\,{p_{n - 1}} + p\left( {1 - p} \right){p_{n - 2}}$$ for all $$n \ge 3.$$ 
Prove by induction on, that $${p_n} = A{\alpha ^n} + B{\beta ^n}$$ for all $$n \ge 1,$$ where $$\alpha $$ and $$\beta $$ are the roots of quadratic equation $${x^2} - \left( {1 - p} \right)x - p\left( {1 - p} \right) = 0$$ and $$A = {{{p^2} + \beta  - 1} \over {\alpha \beta  - {\alpha ^2}}},B = {{{p^2} + \alpha  - 1} \over {\alpha \beta  - {\beta ^2}}}.$$

subject: Mathematics
question_text:
Let $$a,\,b,\,c$$ be possitive real numbers such that $${b^2} - 4ac > 0$$ and let $${\alpha _1} = c.$$ Prove by induction that $${\alpha _{n + 1}} = {{a\alpha _n^2} \over {\left( {{b^2} - 2a\left( {{\alpha _1} + {\alpha _2} + ... + {\alpha _n}} \right)} \right)}}$$ is well-defined and 
$${\alpha _{n + 1}} < {{{\alpha _n}} \over 2}$$ for all $$n = 1,2,....$$ (Here, 'well-defined' means  that the denominator in the expression for $${\alpha _{n + 1}}$$ is not zero.)

subject: Mathematics
micro_topic: Integral And Fractional Part Of A Number
question_text:
For every possitive integer $$n$$, prove that 
$$\sqrt {\left( {4n + 1} \right)}  < \sqrt n  + \sqrt {n + 1}  < \sqrt {4n + 2}.$$ 
Hence or otherwise, prove that $$\left[ {\sqrt n  + \sqrt {\left( {n + 1} \right)} } \right] = \left[ {\sqrt {4n + 1} \,\,} \right],$$
where $$\left[ x \right]$$ denotes the gratest integer not exceeding $$x$$.

subject: Mathematics
micro_topic: Problems Based On Binomial Co Efficient And Collection Of Binomial Co Efficient

subject: Mathematics
micro_topic: Multiple And Sub Multiple Angle Formula
question_text:
In any triangle $$ABC,$$ prove that 
$$$\cot {A \over 2} + \cot {B \over 2} + \cot {C \over 2} = \cot {A \over 2}\cot {B \over 2}\cot {C \over 2}.$$$

subject: Physics
question_text: A thin wire of length $L$ and uniform linear mass density $\rho$ is bent into a circular loop with centre at $O$ as shown. The moment of inertia of the loop about the axis $XX'$ is:
